armed-conflicts.headline: Multiple conflicts escalate globally with significant casualties
armed-conflicts.summary: Massive anti-government protests in Iran have resulted in thousands killed and over 10,000 arrests, with the US considering military options and imposing new tariffs. The war in Ukraine continues to cause civilian casualties and disrupt services due to drone and missile attacks, particularly in Kharkiv. Additionally, clashes in Chad and a drone strike in Sudan led to further deaths and displacements.

regions.name: Tehran, Iran
regions.detail: Massive anti-government protests in Tehran led to thousands of deaths and over 10,000 arrests.

regions.name: Kharkiv, Ukraine
regions.detail: Drone and missile attacks in Kharkiv caused civilian casualties and disrupted essential services.

regions.name: Sinja, Sennar State, Sudan
regions.detail: A drone strike in Sinja, Sennar State, caused deaths and displacement.

regions.name: Korbol, Moyen-Chari, Chad
regions.detail: Clashes in Korbol, Moyen-Chari, caused deaths and displacement.

human-rights.headline: Severe human rights abuses amid crackdowns and migration restrictions
human-rights.summary: Iran is experiencing a severe human rights crisis with thousands of protesters killed and thousands more arrested, alongside internet and texting restrictions. In the US, protests erupted over federal immigration enforcement and the fatal shooting by an ICE agent, leading to lawsuits against the administration. Uganda also implemented a nationwide internet shutdown ahead of elections, raising freedom of expression concerns, while global policies are tightening asylum rules, severely restricting access for refugees.

regions.name: Tehran, Iran
regions.detail: Tehran faced a severe human rights crisis with thousands of protesters killed or arrested.

regions.name: Minneapolis, USA
regions.detail: Protests erupted in Minneapolis over federal immigration enforcement and an ICE agent's fatal shooting.

regions.name: Kampala, Uganda
regions.detail: Kampala initiated a nationwide internet shutdown ahead of elections, sparking human rights concerns.

regions.name: Washington, D.C., USA
regions.detail: Lawsuits were filed against the US administration regarding immigration enforcement and human rights.

regions.name: Tripoli, Libya
regions.detail: Global asylum rules tightened, restricting refugee access in places like Tripoli.

climate-events.headline: Winter storm devastates Gaza; US states seek climate liability shield
climate-events.summary: An intense winter storm in the Gaza Strip caused deaths and destroyed shelters, worsening the dire humanitarian situation. Meanwhile, Republican-led states in the US, including Utah and Oklahoma, are pursuing legislation to create a climate 'liability shield' for the fossil fuel industry, aiming to restrict legal action against oil companies over their environmental impact. The World Food Programme's outlook for 2026 highlights extreme weather as a driver of global hunger.

regions.name: Gaza Strip
regions.detail: An intense winter storm in the Gaza Strip caused deaths and destroyed numerous shelters.

regions.name: Utah, USA
regions.detail: Utah pursued legislation to create a climate 'liability shield' for the fossil fuel industry.

regions.name: Oklahoma, USA
regions.detail: Oklahoma pursued legislation to create a climate 'liability shield' for the fossil fuel industry.

public-health.headline: Health crises intensify in conflict zones; major nurse strike in NYC
public-health.summary: Severe winter conditions in Ukraine are exacerbating a health crisis due to disrupted electricity and heating, limiting access to primary healthcare and medicine. In New York City, nearly 15,000 nurses launched a historic strike over staffing, health benefits, and workplace violence. Additionally, Sudanese refugees in Libya face significant barriers to accessing public health services, while a deepening nutrition emergency is unfolding in North Darfur, Sudan.

regions.name: Kyiv, Ukraine
regions.detail: Severe winter conditions in Kyiv exacerbated a health crisis by disrupting electricity and heating.

regions.name: New York City, USA
regions.detail: Nearly 15,000 nurses in New York City launched a historic strike over staffing and benefits.

regions.name: North Darfur, Sudan
regions.detail: A deepening nutrition emergency unfolded in North Darfur, Sudan.

regions.name: Tripoli, Libya
regions.detail: Sudanese refugees in Tripoli faced significant barriers to accessing public health services.

economic-crises.headline: Global economy resilient but faces slower growth; US markets dip
economic-crises.summary: The World Bank reported the global economy shows resilience but warns the 2020s are set for the weakest growth since the 1960s, with one in four developing economies still poorer than in 2019. US financial markets saw a pullback from record highs due to persistent inflation and mixed corporate earnings. Global inflation is projected to decline, but US inflation remains above target.

regions.name: Washington, D.C., USA
regions.detail: The World Bank in Washington, D.C., reported on global economic resilience and slower growth.

regions.name: New York City, USA
regions.detail: New York City's financial markets saw a pullback from record highs due to inflation.

democratic-erosion.headline: Challenges to democracy persist amid US election rulings, Uganda internet shutdown
democratic-erosion.summary: A federal judge in the US blocked key provisions of President Trump's executive order on elections, reinforcing that states and Congress hold authority over election rules. Uganda implemented a nationwide internet shutdown just two days before its general election, raising concerns about transparency and voter access to information. Mass anti-government protests continued in Iran, posing a significant challenge to the ruling theocracy.

regions.name: Washington, D.C., USA
regions.detail: A federal judge blocked key provisions of President Trump's election executive order.

regions.name: Kampala, Uganda
regions.detail: Kampala enacted a nationwide internet shutdown two days before the general election.

regions.detail: Mass anti-government protests continued in Tehran, challenging the ruling theocracy.

humanitarian.headline: Global hunger crisis deepens; aid efforts strained by funding shortfalls
humanitarian.summary: The World Food Programme warned that over 318 million people face acute food insecurity in 2026, with severe funding shortfalls forcing aid cuts in critical regions. Humanitarian response plans were launched for Ukraine ($2.3 billion needed for 4.1 million people) and South Sudan ($1.5 billion for 4.3 million people) amidst ongoing conflicts and winter conditions. An intense winter storm further exacerbated the humanitarian crisis in Gaza, while 55 million people in West and Central Africa are projected to face crisis levels of hunger due to aid cuts.

regions.name: Rome, Italy
regions.detail: The World Food Programme in Rome warned of a deepening global hunger crisis and aid cuts.

regions.name: Kyiv, Ukraine
regions.detail: A $2.3 billion humanitarian response plan was launched for 4.1 million people in Ukraine.

regions.detail: West and Central Africa projected 55 million people to face crisis levels of hunger due to aid cuts.
